A.A:
An asset is anything that has economic value.
It could be in the form of cash, tangible
properties like land or an object, and even intellectual property.
A bear is an investor who expects company shares or an overall market to go down in value. Meanwhile, a bull is an investor who expects them to increase in value.
Capital is anything that a business uses to run its operations. It can be in the form of cash investments, machinery, land, and non-tangible assets like patents.
Liquidity refers to how easily convertible to cash an asset is without affecting its market price.
For more definitions, visit our website.
